title: Analysts Predict Third GTA 6 Trailer Coming in Late July or Early August
summary: Industry experts are projecting that Rockstar will release the third GTA 6 trailer between late July and early August 2026, with timing potentially aligned with Take-Two's earnings call on August 7. The projection is based on historical marketing patterns from Red Dead Redemption 2 and GTA 5, which released their third trailers roughly four to six months before launch.

title: Fans Speculate August 7 Reveal Date for GTA 6 Trailer
summary: Grand Theft Auto fans believe August 7 will be crucial for the game's marketing push, as it coincides with Take-Two's quarterly earnings call. Some community members suggest there could be two trailers released before launch, with one being cinematic and another focused on gameplay footage that fans have yet to see.

title: PlayStation's Digital-Only Backlash Rivals GTA 6 Social Media Attention
summary: Public reaction to PlayStation's announcement of ending physical disc support in 2028 has generated social media engagement levels that rival GTA 6 discussions. The controversy centers around the impact on future releases including GTA 6, which will ship as a code-in-a-box rather than on physical media.

title: Rockstar Developers Move Toward Unionization Amid Crunch Period
summary: Rockstar Games employees are taking steps to unionize ahead of GTA 6's November 19 launch, citing concerns over crunch culture and working conditions during the final development phase. The effort follows the controversial firing of 31 employees in 2025, with workers seeking protections on overtime, pay transparency, and flexible work arrangements through the IWGB union.

title: GTA 6 Physical Edition Controversy Continues Over Code-Only Release
summary: The debate over GTA 6's physical edition continues as the game will only include a download code rather than a disc, marking the biggest AAA title to fully abandon physical media at launch. Critics argue this gives Sony and digital storefronts complete pricing control with no used game market or retail competition once physical discs phase out.
